enrichment.short_description: Open-source helpdesk platform for eCommerce and multichannel customer support, available self-hosted or as a paid SaaS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UVdesk is an open-source helpdesk for eCommerce support, with a free self-hosted edition and paid SaaS plans from $22/month.

features.title: Multichannel ticketing
features.description: Consolidates inquiries from email, social media, marketplaces, and websites into a single ticket queue.

features.title: eCommerce order integration
features.description: Fetches order data from connected stores so agents can see customer order details inside tickets.

features.title: Knowledge base / support center
features.description: Built-in customer-facing knowledge base and support center for self-serve help content.

features.title: Form builder
features.description: Lets teams create custom support forms for capturing structured customer requests.

features.title: Agent performance tracking
features.description: Reporting on agent activity to monitor support team performance.

features.title: Theme customization
features.description: Customizable support portal themes and branding.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source community edition with unlimited agents
Native integrations with major eCommerce platforms (Shopify, Magento, WooCommerce/WordPress) and marketplaces (Amazon, eBay, Etsy)
SaaS cloud version available for teams that don't want to self-host
Security/compliance certifications claimed for the cloud offering (ISO 27001, ISO 9001, SOC 2 Type 2)
cons: Paid SaaS plans require a minimum of 2 agents
Advanced features (full portal customization, unlimited mailboxes/knowledgebase) are reserved for the higher Pro tier
Self-hosted deployment requires PHP server setup and maintenance

faqs.question: Is UVdesk free?
faqs.answer: Yes. UVdesk offers a free, self-hosted open-source community edition with unlimited agents, plus a free SaaS tier with standard features.

faqs.question: Does UVdesk integrate with eCommerce platforms?
faqs.answer: Yes, UVdesk supports integrations with Shopify, Magento, OpenCart, PrestaShop, WordPress, Amazon, eBay, Etsy, and Flipkart.

faqs.question: What does the paid SaaS version cost?
faqs.answer: Paid SaaS plans start at $22/month (or $192/year) for Basic, requiring a minimum of 2 agents, with a Pro plan at $36/month for larger teams.

faqs.question: Can I self-host UVdesk?
faqs.answer: Yes, an open-source community skeleton is available for self-hosted deployment on your own server.

use_cases.title: eCommerce customer support
use_cases.description: Online stores and marketplace sellers managing order-related support inquiries across multiple sales channels.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted helpdesk
use_cases.description: Organizations that want a free, fully controlled helpdesk deployed on their own infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Technical teams and cost-conscious support operations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UVdesk?
paragraphs: UVdesk is a helpdesk and customer support platform built by Webkul, aimed at eCommerce businesses and marketplace sellers who need to manage support tickets across many channels.
It is offered both as a free, open-source self-hosted PHP application and as a paid SaaS product with tiered plans, giving teams a choice between full control and managed hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who UVdesk is for
paragraphs: UVdesk suits online stores and marketplace sellers who need order-aware support tickets, as well as budget-conscious teams that want a self-hosted, open-source helpdesk they can run on their own servers.

name: Peppermint
skip_reason: Product appears discontinued: the official website domain peppermint.sh no longer resolves (DNS failure), and the source GitHub repository (Peppermint-Lab/peppermint) is archived and read-only as of July 2026, with the latest release dating to November 2024.

enrichment.short_description: Helpdesk platform with ticketing, live chat, and knowledge base, offered self-hosted, on private cloud, or as SaaS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Helpy is a helpdesk with ticketing, live chat, and a knowledge base, deployable self-hosted, on private cloud, or as SaaS; self-hosted starts at $40/seat/month.

features.title: Multi-channel ticketing
features.description: Email integration with ticket tracking and assignment across channels.

features.title: Live chat
features.description: Real-time customer chat with auto-assignment and bot support.

features.title: Self-serve knowledge base
features.description: Customizable knowledge base with access controls for customer self-service.

features.title: SSO and security controls
features.description: Supports SAML, OAuth, and LDAP single sign-on plus data masking and role-based permissions.

features.title: Quick reply templates
features.description: Canned responses and one-click conversion of replies into knowledge base articles.

pros: Flexible deployment: self-hosted/on-premises, private cloud (AWS/Azure), or turnkey SaaS
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Open-source community edition available on GitHub
Enterprise security options (SSO, data masking) aimed at regulated industries
cons: Self-hosted pricing requires an annual contract at $40 per seat per month
Cloud-hosted plan pricing is not published on the main pricing page
The open-source GitHub repository's most recent release dates to 2020, indicating the free community edition may lag behind the commercial product

faqs.question: Can I try Helpy for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Helpy offers a 14-day free trial that does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: How much does self-hosted Helpy cost?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted plan is $40 per seat per month, billed under an annual contract, and includes every feature Helpy offers.

faqs.question: Is there an open-source version of Helpy?
faqs.answer: Yes, an open-source core is available on GitHub, though its most recent public release was in 2020.

faqs.question: Where is Helpy based?
faqs.answer: Helpy's headquarters is listed at 2701 N. Thanksgiving Way, Suite 100, Lehi, Utah, USA.

use_cases.title: Regulated-industry support
use_cases.description: Teams needing on-premises or private-cloud data control for compliance reasons.
use_cases.audience: Financial services, healthcare, government, and education organizations

use_cases.title: Self-hosted support desk
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting full infrastructure control over their helpdesk deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Helpy?
paragraphs: Helpy is a customer support helpdesk that combines ticketing, live chat, and a self-serve knowledge base into a single platform.
It can be deployed self-hosted on private hardware, on a private cloud (AWS, Azure, or similar), or used as a turnkey SaaS product, with an open-source community edition also available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Helpy is for
paragraphs: Helpy targets organizations that prioritize data control and compliance, such as financial services, healthcare, education, government, defense, and telecom, where SSO and self-hosting matter.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source support ticket system used by millions worldwide, with an optional paid hosted cloud version.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: osTicket is a free, open-source ticketing system for support teams, with a paid hosted cloud edition called SupportSystem.

features.title: Multi-channel ticket routing
features.description: Routes inquiries created via email, web forms, and API into a unified ticket queue.

features.title: Intelligent ticket filtering
features.description: Configurable rules and filters route tickets to the right agents or teams.

features.title: Configurable help topics
features.description: Custom help topics and forms tailored to different types of requests.

features.title: Customer support portal
features.description: Self-service portal where customers can submit and track tickets.

features.title: SLA management
features.description: Service Level Agreement rules to track and enforce response/resolution times.

features.title: Dashboard reporting
features.description: Reporting and analytics dashboard for ticket and agent activity.

pros: Completely free, open-source (GPL-2.0) with no cost to self-host
Large, established user base — reported 5+ million users and 15,000+ businesses
Actively maintained with regular releases (v1.18.4 released 2026)
API access for integrating ticket creation with other systems
cons: Self-hosted edition requires you to install, host, and maintain your own server
Cloud-hosted SupportSystem pricing is not published on the main site
Advanced support requires purchasing commercial support/professional services from Enhancesoft

faqs.question: Is osTicket really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted osTicket software is free and open source under the GPL-2.0 license.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of osTicket?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enhancesoft offers SupportSystem, a cloud-hosted version of osTicket with a 30-day free trial that requires no credit card.

faqs.question: Who maintains osTicket?
faqs.answer: osTicket is developed and commercially supported by Enhancesoft, which also offers professional services.

faqs.question: Can osTicket integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, osTicket supports ticket creation via email, web forms, and API.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted support ticketing
use_cases.description: Organizations that want a free, self-managed ticketing system with full data control.
use_cases.audience: IT, education, healthcare, government, and nonprofit support teams

use_cases.title: Managed cloud support desk
use_cases.description: Teams that want osTicket's ticketing without managing their own server.
use_cases.audience: Small and mid-sized businesses using SupportSystem

features: 30-day free trial, no credit card required

long_form_blocks.heading: What is osTicket?
paragraphs: osTicket is an open-source support ticket system that consolidates inquiries from email, web forms, and the API into a single, unified ticket queue.
It is free to self-host under the GPL-2.0 license, and its maker, Enhancesoft, also sells a hosted cloud version called SupportSystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who osTicket is for
paragraphs: osTicket suits organizations across education, healthcare, technology, government, and nonprofits that want a free, widely-used ticketing system they can self-host, or a hosted alternative if they don't want to manage a server.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, enterprise-grade ticketing and workflow system used for over 20 years, with a paid Cloud RT hosting option.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Request Tracker (RT) is an open-source ticketing and workflow system with 400+ extensions, self-hosted or via paid Cloud RT hosting.

features.title: Email-based ticketing
features.description: Manages tickets from multiple email addresses with tracking and assignment.

features.title: Visual workflow builder
features.description: Configure ticket lifecycles and workflows visually.

features.title: Knowledge base (Articles)
features.description: Stores FAQs and canned responses as reusable articles.

features.title: Asset management
features.description: Tracks tangible and intangible assets within RT via the RT Assets module.

features.title: REST 2 API
features.description: REST API with Zapier integration support for automation.

features.title: SLA management
features.description: Business-hours-aware SLA tracking and reporting.

features.title: Incident response module
features.description: RT Incident Response (RTIR) extension for security/incident workflows.

pros: Open source with over 400 free community-built extensions
Long track record — in active use for over 20 years
Actively maintained with recent releases (rt-6.0.3 in 2026)
REST API and Zapier support for integrations
cons: Self-hosted setup requires technical installation and maintenance
Cloud RT pricing is not published and requires contacting the vendor
Interface and setup can feel dated compared to modern SaaS helpdesks

faqs.question: Is Request Tracker free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted, open-source version of RT is free to use under the GPL-2.0 license.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cloud RT is a paid, subscription-based hosted version, with a free trial available.

faqs.question: Does RT have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, RT provides a REST 2 API with Zapier integration support.

faqs.question: What other products come with RT?
faqs.answer: Bestpractical/Request Tracker also offers RT Incident Response (RTIR), RT Assets, and RT FormTools.

use_cases.title: IT and support ticketing
use_cases.description: Teams tracking support requests, tasks, and change management through customizable workflows.

use_cases.title: Security incident response
use_cases.description: Organizations using the RTIR extension to manage security incidents.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Request Tracker?
paragraphs: Request Tracker (RT) is an open-source, enterprise-grade issue and ticket tracking system that has been in use for over 20 years, built by Best Practical / requesttracker.com.
It can be self-hosted for free, extended with 400+ community extensions, or run as a paid managed service called Cloud RT.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Request Tracker is for
paragraphs: RT suits IT departments, helpdesks, and security operations teams that need a highly customizable, self-hostable ticketing system without vendor lock-in.

enrichment.short_description: 100% open-source, license-free service management platform for IT, customer support, and enterprise service teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OTOBO is a free, open-source service management platform for ITSM, customer service, and enterprise service management.

features.title: IT Service Management (ITSM)
features.description: ITIL-aligned processes for delivering IT services.

features.title: Customer Service Management (CSM)
features.description: Multi-channel customer support ticketing and communication.

features.title: Enterprise Service Management (ESM)
features.description: Orchestrates services across multiple internal or external providers.

features.title: Process automation
features.description: Customizable service process automation and self-service capabilities.

features.title: Multi-channel communication
features.description: Supports handling requests across multiple communication channels.

pros: 100% open source and free, with no licensing cost
GPL-licensed with source available on GitHub (RotherOSS/otobo)
Used by large organizations including Siemens, Infineon, and NATO
Optional managed hosting available through German data centers for teams wanting data residency
cons: Self-hosting requires technical setup and Perl-based server maintenance
Consulting, training, support contracts, and managed hosting are paid add-ons with pricing not published
Smaller GitHub community (roughly 330 stars) compared to some other open-source helpdesk projects

faqs.question: Is OTOBO free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OTOBO is 100% open source and license-free to self-host.

faqs.question: Who develops OTOBO?
faqs.answer: OTOBO is developed by Rother OSS GmbH, founded by a former ((OTRS)) Community Edition team member, as a fork focused on staying free and community-driven.

faqs.question: Does OTOBO offer paid support?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rother OSS offers optional paid consulting, training, support contracts, and managed hosting in German data centers.

faqs.question: Can OTOBO handle IT service management?
faqs.answer: Yes, OTOBO includes ITSM capabilities aligned with ITIL best practices, alongside customer service and enterprise service management modules.

use_cases.title: IT service management
use_cases.description: IT departments implementing ITIL-aligned service delivery and ticketing.

use_cases.title: Enterprise service orchestration
use_cases.description: Organizations coordinating services across multiple internal departments or external providers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OTOBO?
paragraphs: OTOBO is a web-based, modular service management platform covering IT service management, customer service, and enterprise service management, released as free, license-free open source software.
It is developed by Rother OSS GmbH, which also sells optional consulting, training, support contracts, and managed hosting for organizations that don't want to self-host.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OTOBO is for
paragraphs: OTOBO suits IT departments and enterprises that need ITIL-aligned service management and want a fully free, self-hostable alternative to commercial service desk software, with the option of paid managed hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source IT service management and asset/CMDB platform, self-hosted for free or via paid GLPI Network Cloud plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GLPI is an open-source ITSM and asset management platform, free to self-host or available via GLPI Network Cloud from €19/agent/month.

features.title: Helpdesk and ticket management
features.description: Manages support tickets alongside IT assets and infrastructure.

features.title: Asset and inventory management
features.description: Tracks hardware, software, and other assets across the organization.

features.title: CMDB
features.description: Configuration Management Database for tracking infrastructure relationships.

features.title: Agentless cloud inventory
features.description: Scans and inventories cloud resources without installing agents.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Automates recurring IT service and asset workflows.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: iOS and Android mobile applications for on-the-go access.

pros: Free, open-source core (GPL-3.0) actively maintained with recent releases (v11.0.8, 2026)
Large established community with 6,100+ GitHub stars
Managed cloud hosting available (GLPI Network Cloud) if self-hosting isn't wanted
Mobile apps for iOS and Android
cons: Cloud plans are priced per IT agent, which can scale up in cost for larger teams
Advanced plugin tiers (Standard, Advanced) require paid GLPI Network subscriptions starting at €100/month
Self-hosted deployment requires server setup and maintenance

faqs.question: Is GLPI free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI's core platform is free and open source (GPL-3.0) to self-host.

faqs.question: How much does GLPI Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: GLPI Network Public Cloud starts at €19/month per IT agent; Private Cloud starts at €21/month per IT agent (minimum 25 agents).

faqs.question: Does GLPI have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI's cloud offering includes a 45-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does GLPI have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI offers mobile applications for iOS and Android.

use_cases.title: IT asset and service management
use_cases.description: IT teams managing tickets, assets, and infrastructure in one platform.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud ITSM
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting GLPI without managing their own servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GLPI?
paragraphs: GLPI is an open-source IT service management platform that unifies helpdesk ticketing, asset inventory, and a configuration management database (CMDB) in one tool.
It's free to self-host under GPL-3.0, and its maintainer Teclib' also sells managed GLPI Network Cloud hosting priced per IT agent, plus paid plugin tiers for self-hosted deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GLPI is for
paragraphs: GLPI suits mid-to-enterprise IT teams that need combined ticketing and asset/CMDB management, whether they prefer to self-host for free or pay for managed cloud hosting.

enrichment.short_description: French open-source ITSM and CMDB platform with a free Community tier and paid Atelier/Forge subscriptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: iTop is an open-source ITSM and CMDB platform by Combodo, free for up to 10 agents with paid Atelier and Forge tiers for larger teams.

features.title: ITIL-certified service management
features.description: Request, incident, problem, and change management processes aligned with ITIL.

features.title: CMDB
features.description: Fully customizable Configuration Management Database for infrastructure visibility and dependency mapping.

features.title: Low-code customization
features.description: Customize workflows and data models without developer expertise.

features.title: SLA tracking
features.description: Activity monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.

features.title: Automation
features.description: Automates repetitive service management tasks.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Extensible via a hub of community and vendor add-ons.

pros: Free Community tier supports up to 10 agents
Open source (AGPL-3.0) and actively maintained on GitHub, with recent releases (v3.2.3, 2026)
Available as SaaS or self-hosted/on-premise
Low-code customization reduces reliance on developers
cons: Community tier is capped at 10 agents; larger teams need paid Atelier (up to 50 agents) or Forge (50+) tiers
Pricing for paid tiers is not published and requires contacting Combodo
Self-hosted deployment requires PHP server setup and maintenance

faqs.question: Is iTop free?
faqs.answer: Yes, iTop's Community edition is free and supports up to 10 agents; it is open source under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: What are the paid iTop tiers?
faqs.answer: Atelier (up to 50 agents) and Forge (50+ agents) are paid tiers for teams needing more capacity and industrialized ITSM.

faqs.question: Can iTop be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, iTop supports both self-hosted/on-premise deployment and a SaaS option.

faqs.question: Who develops iTop?
faqs.answer: iTop is developed and sponsored by Combodo, a French company.

use_cases.title: Small-team ITSM
use_cases.description: Small IT teams (up to 10 agents) using the free Community edition.

use_cases.title: Enterprise ITSM and CMDB
use_cases.description: Larger organizations needing industrialized, customized service management with paid support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is iTop?
paragraphs: iTop is an open-source IT service management and CMDB platform developed by the French company Combodo, offering ITIL-aligned processes, low-code customization, and a plugin ecosystem.
It's free for small teams under the Community tier (up to 10 agents) and available in paid Atelier and Forge tiers, deployable as SaaS or self-hosted/on-premise.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who iTop is for
paragraphs: iTop suits IT teams of any size seeking a customizable, ITIL-aligned ITSM and CMDB platform, from small teams on the free tier to larger enterprises needing industrialized service management.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source IT asset management system, free to self-host or available as paid managed cloud hosting from $39.99/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snipe-IT is an open-source IT asset management system, free to self-host or available as managed cloud hosting from $39.99/month.

features.title: Asset tracking and inventory
features.description: Tracks IT and other organizational assets in place of spreadsheets.

features.title: REST API
features.description: API for custom integrations and automation.

features.title: Security controls
features.description: Bcrypt password hashing, brute-force prevention, two-factor authentication, and HTTPS-only cookies.

features.title: Frequent releases
features.description: New releases roughly every few weeks.

features.title: Customizable asset types
features.description: Track more than just IT equipment by customizing asset categories.

pros: Free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) and self-hostable with unlimited users and assets
Large active community: 14,100+ GitHub stars, 291 releases, 330+ contributors
Managed cloud hosting available across 12 global regions on AWS with a 99.99% SLA
REST API for building custom integrations
cons: Cloud hosting plans add meaningful monthly cost ($39.99–$249.99+/month) on top of the free self-hosted option
Automated upgrades and priority support require a paid hosting plan
Self-hosted deployment requires server setup and maintenance

faqs.question: Is Snipe-IT free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipe-IT is free and open source (AGPL-3.0) if you self-host it.

faqs.question: How much does Snipe-IT cloud hosting cost?
faqs.answer: Managed cloud hosting starts at $39.99/month (Basic) and scales up to $249.99/month for Dedicated hosting, with larger dedicated tiers available.

faqs.question: Does Snipe-IT have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipe-IT provides a REST API for custom integrations and automations.

faqs.question: How large is the Snipe-IT community?
faqs.answer: The project has 14,100+ GitHub stars, 291 releases, and 330+ contributors.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted asset management
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting free, self-managed IT asset tracking with full data control.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud asset tracking
use_cases.description: Teams wanting Snipe-IT without running their own server.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snipe-IT?
paragraphs: Snipe-IT is an open-source IT asset management system built to replace spreadsheet-based inventory tracking with a dedicated platform for managing hardware, software licenses, and other assets.
It's free to self-host under AGPL-3.0, and its maker also sells managed cloud hosting plans across 12 global regions on AWS, starting at $39.99/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snipe-IT is for
paragraphs: Snipe-IT suits organizations of any size that want affordable, non-vendor-locked-in asset tracking, whether they prefer to self-host for free or pay for managed cloud hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source Asset Management, DCIM, and CMDB system for data centers and back offices, maintained by Allegro.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ralph is a free, open-source Asset Management, DCIM, and CMDB system for tracking data center and back-office hardware.

features.title: Asset purchase and lifecycle tracking
features.description: Tracks hardware purchases and their lifecycle from acquisition to retirement.

features.title: Flexible asset lifecycle workflow
features.description: Configurable workflow system for moving assets through lifecycle stages.

features.title: Data center and back-office support
features.description: Handles both data center hardware and general back-office equipment.

features.title: Data center visualization
features.description: Built-in visualization tools for data center layouts.

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Established project with 2,500+ GitHub stars
Recent releases continuing into 2026, showing ongoing maintenance
Purpose-built for combined DCIM/CMDB/asset-management use cases
cons: Allegro maintains the project on a "sources only" basis without guaranteed support or issue response
No commercial support contract or managed hosting option was found
Requires self-hosting and technical setup (Python/Django stack)

faqs.question: Is Ralph free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ralph is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: Who maintains Ralph?
faqs.answer: Ralph is maintained by Allegro, which publishes the source code on GitHub but offers it on a sources-only basis without guaranteed support.

faqs.question: What does Ralph track?
faqs.answer: Ralph tracks data center and back-office hardware assets, including purchase history and lifecycle stage, with data center visualization tools.

faqs.question: Is Ralph actively maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GitHub repository shows releases continuing into 2026.

use_cases.title: Data center asset tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations tracking hardware assets and lifecycle across data centers.
use_cases.audience: Data center operations and infrastructure teams

use_cases.title: Back-office equipment inventory
use_cases.description: IT teams managing non-data-center hardware inventory.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ralph?
paragraphs: Ralph is a free, open-source Asset Management, DCIM, and CMDB system for tracking data center and back-office hardware, originally built and maintained by Allegro.
It's released under the Apache 2.0 license and is self-hosted; Allegro maintains it on a sources-only basis without guaranteed community support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ralph is for
paragraphs: Ralph suits infrastructure and data center teams that need a free, self-hostable tool for tracking hardware assets and their lifecycle alongside data center visualization.
